title: Meow Homestay: Wake up by the lake in Taiping (Perak) at this family-friendly nature retreat
content:
Written by Shannon Osztonits

Read more:

10 Affordable weekend girls’ getaways in Janda Baik and Bukit Tinggi (1 hour from Kuala Lumpur) with private pool for big-groups (6-30 pax)!

Are you and the family desperate for a taste of nature (especially considering the times)? Feel as if you’ve not only escaped city-life, but kind of like you’re not really in Malaysia anymore at this nature retreat in Taiping!

Done up in a retro-vintage vibe, the open and spacious set up is ideal for families or large groups travelling together sleeping up to 16 guests - so the whole famdam can come along!

The entire house is fully equipped – kitchen, bbq pit, living space and all, consisting of four levels, and we love the mix of old and new making it interesting for all ages. (Our favourite room is the full glass window one overlooking the lake! Dreamy for mom and dad.)

And as if the house itself wasn’t epic enough, there’s an observatory deck upstairs overlooking the lake - perfect for sundowners, watching the stars, morning yoga sessions or chilling out in the fresh air! Spend the day lazying around the lake, swimming, kayaking and there are also bicycles and boats for all guests to use.

Lastly, we need to give a shout out to Mother Nature herself, which in this particular location is just breath-taking (do go exploring and don’t forget to check out the outdoor tree by the lake filled with wild orchids, you can’t miss it).

Taiping Meow Homestay 妙艺湖景民宿

title: Promised Garden: Whimsical bus cafe in Johor with everything floral and pink
content:
Written by Grace Oktaviani

Read more:

Cafe hopping in JB: 13 Unique themed cafes near shopping and dining hotspots (City Square, KSL, Mount Austin, Bukit Indah)

We love a good weekend cafe-hopping, don’t we? Well, astonishingly, Johor is filled with a gazillion of Instagrammable cafes that don’t only call for brunch but also for your OOTD moments However, we understand each and everyone’s concern about eating out at a four-walled cafe lately; so check out this garden cafe instead!

Plenty of space between tables (that means a A+ for social distancing), Promised Garden is where you can take a seat among the blossom trees! The space is adorned with cute little vintage decor like a pink retro bus and fairy-tale looking chairs! Call it girly, but we’re sure you’ll be able to drag your Instagram boyfriend here too!

Besides, if your friends don’t come for the Instagram factor, they’ll surely stay for the delicious food on offer – from comforting desserts like Sweet Potato Red Bean Tang Shui to classic drinks like Milk Tea. And if you’re coming with an empty stomach, they also offer light snacks like dim sums as well as heavier meals such as Fried Chicken Chop Nasi Lemak and Mala Ramen.

Note: You can also book the entire place for your wedding or other special occasions! Please contact them directly for more information.

There’s no words for me to properly describe this unique cafe except it being picturesque. Pineyard cafe is a definite special find with its pine wood floors and pillars to its minimalistic steel white arches – this place oozes cosiness and warmth within the chilly forest of Janda Baik.

You can have your coffee indoors by their bar or maybe outside on the grass near a pond. Wherever you choose, every corner is just dreamy. Their range of food isn’t too bad either; mostly Western dishes, but you can opt for their all-day breakfast as well!

So come on by, and don’t forget to bring your cameras with you!

title: Waronk - Riverside food court in Melaka with more than 1000 dishes and live music
content:
Written by Nur Batrisya

There's lots to see and much to discover whenever I take a trip down to Melaka. From their historical landmarks to their colourful graffiti art, I’m in a constant state of wonder for what this place brings. But what makes it more thrilling is their food scene.

I’ve only been to Melaka a few times, so when I heard about Waronk, I was super intrigued. A delightful gastronomical experience always comes hand-in-hand with pleasant views and delicious meals. For Waronk, it checks both boxes!

Located just along Sungai Melaka, this two-storey eatery has people flocking over for their delicious variety of foods and views. Whether it’s spicy Asam Pedas or juicy chendol, you can indulge yourself in any of their Malay, Chinese or Western cuisine (They serve up to 200 dishes to be exact) and all of it can be enjoyed by the riverside!

Just a heads up though, this place can get pretty packed. People would not only come here for their food, but for their fun live bands as well. Good music and good food equals good vibes! And watch out for parking spots too as they are limited!

Well I don’t know about you, but I’m certainly excited to make my way over to this joint when I come down to Melaka, what about you?

WARONK

Address: No.118, Lorong Hang Jebat , 75200 Melaka 75300 Contact: +6017-235 9322 Opening hours: Daily, 11.45 am-12 am

title: Blue Zone Cafe: Romantic lakeside seafood restaurant in Kuala Lumpur
content:
Written by Nur Batrisya

Within the concrete city of Kuala Lumpur, who would ever think that you’d be able to find a seafood restaurant that has the same essence as ones we prefer to enjoy by the sea or by the lake. Yet, day by day, this city never ceases to amaze me.

Hidden just along the outskirts of Pandan Perdana is a small lakeside seafood restaurant that’s famous for their delicious Thai cuisine, not to mention, wallet-friendly too! This place also oozes a cosy romantic atmosphere with their twinkling fairy lights with a touch of Kampung feels. Their cute little boat also adds a nice decorational touch to the atmosphere!

Over here, many people raved about their Mantis Prawn with Salted Egg (Har you) for it’s tasty balance of slightly sweet salted egg and crispy texture to it. And of course, you can’t go to a Thai Seafood restaurant without actually ordering their Tom Yam set! Although there’s plenty more recommended dishes, you’ll need to come and see it for yourself, and don’t worry bout’ the portions either. They aren’t greedy.

PS. They don’t take reservations, only walk-ins! And this restaurant is also Non-Halal.

Blue Zone Cafe

title: Cove Aerobar - Dine in the sky near Kuala Lumpur with breathtaking views (affordable version!)
content:
Have you ever been swept off your feet? Like ‘literally’ being swept off your feet? (in a good way of course!)

The concept of sky dining isn’t new to Malaysia, and if you noticed, there’s a well-known one in the heart of KL. From what I’ve heard, their 6-course meal is superb, but their price tag? Super hefty. What if I told you that there’s another, just as an equally exciting dine-in-the-sky experience for you at just RM 65?

Suspended in the air about 35 metres high, not only will you get to indulge in good food, but in lovely views all around, especially if you catch it around sunset! Some say it wasn’t as scary as it looks in photos, and if anything, it’s worth every penny! If you’re someone who’s afraid of heights, try not to look down but focus on your food, views and great company! This ride only serves one meal and you’ll be up in the air for around 10 minutes.

But if you’re still in for some more fun, they also have other attractions at Discovery Park as well! They have drift karts to get your adrenaline rush going or you can even dip in Malaysia’s first diving & largest salt water pool! Other facilities are still in the works though, but I can’t wait to see what’s more to come!

Cove Aerobar

Price fees: At RM 65 per ride Address: Persiaran Cove Sentral, Bandar Gamuda Cove, 42700 Banting, Selangor Darul Ehsan Contact: +6012 663 0155 Opening hours: Wednesday to Monday, 11am - 8pm (closed on Tuesdays)

title: Rumah Sungai: Hidden affordable riverside hotel in Perak perfect for families
content:
Written by Nur Batrisya

If there’s one thing (well, out of the many things) I love about Malaysia, is her abundance of nature that decorates our beautiful country. When life gets hectic, I always love going on a nature retreat to anywhere far and away from the noise, and oftentimes, I find peace in places that are near waterfalls or rivers.

If you’re someone like me who enjoys dipping their toes in fresh river water in the morning, then you might like this hidden Riverside Hotel in Perak. Rumah Sungai Perak has grabbed the attention of many locals, mostly due to its unique concept that comes with such an affordable price. This humble yet rustic stay is perfect for those who are longing for a simple kampung lifestyle.

Over here, you have plenty of things to do, from fishing to jungle trekking, or even just cooling off in the fresh clear river water (has a fun slide too!). An added bonus, they even offer a fabulous massage, JUST for the ladies, at RM 50 per session (how cheap is that!) Nothing beats a classic massage from a local masseuse than the ones in the city which can be rough on your body sometimes…

Most people who visited enjoyed how warm and friendly the host of this place was. Their meals are delicious, their stay is comfortable with rooms fitting up to 3 pax. All you need is good company and nothing else!

Due to popular demand, their rooms are fully booked till the end of the year. But no worries, 2021 slots are still open and you can start booking from 1st November 2020! So save this down!

Rumah Sungai

Price: From RM 180 per night for 3 pax (breakfast & lunch inc.) Address: Kg Lata Papan, Kampung Kuak, 33400 Lenggong, Perak Contact: +6011-5228 1897

title: Emperor Villa: Hidden mountain villa and restaurant in Penang that brings you ancient China
content:
Written by Grace Oktaviani

Travel back to ancient China at this hidden mountain villa in Penang, where trees and fresh air abound. Located on a hiking hill just 30 minutes from Georgetown, find yourself basking in nature’s calm vibes when you spend a night at this rustic village newly opened last year.

Wake up to the sounds of birds chirping and leaves rustling in the wind as you stay in one of the huts on stilts or wooden traditional villas.

Make your experience even better by joining one of the on-site activities, from harvesting ripe starfruit and cucumbers to making ceramics – a great way to relieve stress! You can even buy durians here during the durian season!

Or for the adventurous ones, you can also join their half-day trip that consists of hiking, swimming, a karaoke session and visiting a temple and starfruit farm (RM 88 per pax; min. 4 people).

But if you’re just looking to chill, head over to their authentic Hakka Restaurant for their Black Sweet Vinegar Pork Trotter and Stir-Fried Yam Bean, or their Mountain Stream Water Pool, where everything is green (great for mental detox!).

Remember to bring water before you start your journey to this villa and restaurant as it’ll take you approximately 1 hour to hike up.

Emperor Villa

Price: Hotel from RM488 per night | Kaya toast from RM 2.50 Address: 496,Off Jalan Kenari, Mukim 10,Sungai Ara, 11900 Bayan Lepas, Penang, Malaysia Contact: +60 16 558 5127

title: 99 Wonderland Park: Dinosaur park in Kuala Lumpur with petting zoo and LED coloured fountain
content:
Written by Nur Batrisya

Growing up, I’ve always been a fan of Jurassic Park: Lost World because of the dinosaurs and wondered how they’d look in real life. However, most dinosaurs we see are in small exhibits and sometimes, cost a pretty penny for just a 20-minute view. But at 99 Wonderland Park, you’ll get all the views and so much more at such a cheap price!

First main attraction for the kids (and adults like myself) is two huge 3.5-metre Tyrannosaurs displayed in the park for us to geek out our inner paleontologist! But if you want to check out a real life reptile, you can also visit their cousins at the crocodile exhibit nearby!

They have tons of other live animals as well, from sun bears to Malayan tapirs and more! One of their highlights is also the Panda Street, that is filled with – you guessed it, PANDAS! But wait, there’s more, by night, the whole park would be filled with colourful LED lights, from the zoos to their mini boats and trains. The kids would love it!

You can also take an evening stroll along their Magical Bridge, also known as a ‘lovelock bridge’. So for all you sweethearts out there, bring a lock and pen to seal the deal here. To add some more luck into your life, walk along their Golden Waterfall trail. Since gold is believed to symbolise fortune and good luck, you can have a good 115-metre long walk to manifest all of that!

Top off your day with their 99 cruise for a cool, chill breeze ride around the park, and explore the natural habitats around or check out their beautiful LED-lit Eiffel Tower!

99 WonderlandPark

Entrance fees: Weekdays, RM15 (daytime entry) & RM25 (night time entry) for adults, RM10 (daytime entry) & RM15 (night time entry) for children Weekends & PH, RM25 (daytime entry) & RM35 (night time entry) for adults, RM15 (daytime entry) & RM20 (night time entry) for children Address: Jalan 13a, Pusat Bandar Utara Selayang, 68100 Batu Caves, Selangor Contact: +603 6128 3799 Opening hours: Tuesday - Friday, 10am - 4.30pm, 5pm - 12am; Saturday, Sunday & PH, 10am - 4.30pm, 5pm - 12am/1am (Closed on Mondays)

title: Bala’s Holiday Chalet: Stay at Cameron Highlands’ little English home with European weathers and more!
content:
There's good reason why Cameron Highlands is also known as the Little England of Malaysia, due to the handful of beautiful old English country homes, some of which were built during the British colonial era as home-away-from-home retreats for British soldiers and expats who were based in Malaysia at that time.

As for The Bala's Holiday Chalet, it opened initially as a boarding school in 1934 for children of European expats. After the school closed down, its present owners, Mr K. Balakrishnan (a local) and Peter Blumbach (a German businessman), bought over the property.

They turned it into this guesthouse you see today, retaining its original Tudor decor, that even when former students returned for leisure visits, they could still recognise the old music room, dining room and more.

Considerably more affordable than other similar-themed guesthouses in Cameron Highlands, The Bala's Holiday Chalet (now also known as Planters Country Hotel) is a pretty value-for-money stay if you want to indulge in old English countrysides, traditional afternoon teas and a comfortable stay without forking out too much!

The Bala's Holiday Chalet Cameron Highlands

title: Heahtitude: Stay by the riverside in Cameron Highlands at this 90s’-inspired bed and breakfast!
content:
Written by Nur Batrisya

Do you need an escape to somewhere further from KL yet close to home? Come check out this riverside 90s inspired bed and breakfast homestay residing on the hills of Cameron Highlands.

The last time I went to Cameron Highlands, my friends and I never really got to explore the place so much, but I remembered how cool the wind was, and how peaceful it was compared to Genting.

Maybe because it’s less crowded and less congested without cars on weekends. So when I came across Heahtitude (pronounced like the Cantonese slang) I couldn’t help but be drawn to the simplicity of its beauty.

Imagine waking up, sipping hot tea in the cold morning with a breathtaking view of a mountain and river. One can only imagine how peaceful that must be... This place is perfect for backpackers or travellers who won’t mind sharing a space with strangers. You can exchange interesting stories or have thoughtful convos with your BFFs at night by their Riverside Patio that overlooks the illuminated flower farms.

The ease of comfort with this place most probably has to do with their unique yet wholesome interior concept, inspired by the vintage charm and simpler times back in the 90s. Sometimes we all need a little something old, to appreciate the new...

Heahtitude

title: Arte S Penang: Unique futuristic stay in Penang with a jacuzzi and sky lounge overlooking the island
content:
Written by Nur Batrisya

Are you looking for a unique staycation around Penang? Check out this Airbnb located 18 minutes from Georgetown!

When it comes to unique places to vacay in, I usually go for ones that have a relaxing view of the surroundings around me with a really good architectural design behind it. So when I came across Arte S Penang, the first thing that caught my eye was their jacuzzi and how it was enclosed within an almost dome-like shell with wide windows, sharing a city view of Penang Island. It feels like you have the whole view for yourself, and you can unwind with a good drink on the side!

Aside from its jacuzzi, their futuristic sky lounge is perfect for those nights when you prefer to sit back and take in the night sky view around you. There’s also a mini sitting area with leaf-covered walls and bean bags with a view of the city as well. I’d make this place my reading spot if you'd ask me!

There’s tons of other cool facilities as well such as a gym, sauna, BBQ pits and more, so you have a variety of chilled activities you can do with your friends. This homestay has a balcony with a sea view complete with 3 bedrooms that could fit in up to 8 people! From just RM34.40 per pax, this place sounds like a steal, doesn’t it?!

Book your spot now, and let the fun staycation begin!

content:

Every now and then (if not always), don’t we love staying at an Instagrammable hotel?

title: Tree Monkey Restaurant: Dine with a scenic view in Penang at this oceanview treehouse restaurant
content:
Take your dining experience to a whole new level on Penang Island at this cosy treehouse restaurant, where you can enjoy classic Thai cuisine (think delicious pineapple fried rice, Phad Thai, Tom Yum soup, mango sticky rice and more!) while overlooking the sea.

Located right beside Tropical Spice Garden, simply take the stairs up and you'll find yourself comfortably among the trees. It's cool and shady up here under the trees, the perfect place to chill with your family and friends at sunset!

P.S. There's a reason why this place is named Tree Monkey, as you might spot some monkeys around, chilling among the trees around 5pm! (Just remember to keep a distance from them and don't feed them. They wouldn't leave you alone if you do the same.) Bring mosquito repellent too!

title: Live the Crazy Rich Asian life at Penang’s iconic Cheong Fatt Tze Mansion (The Blue Mansion)
content:
Have you ever stayed in a 19th Century old mansion before? Well we’ve got something special for you! You might have seen this place in Crazy Rich Asians.

Cheong Fatt Tze, or known as The Blue Mansion is a beautiful boutique hotel that’s located in Penang, not too far from the Penang State Museum. This gorgeous classic built was inspired by the ol’ Hakka and Teochew architectural styles, but don’t be fooled, its interior takes a modern twist with high ceilings that’s decorated with antique 1950s furnishings, keeping the vintage style of the mansion on point. Fun fact: some furnishings were also inherited from Cheong Fatt Tze’s family as well!

Wouldn’t it be a nice time travel in the most comforting way, where you can explore the history of the walls around you?

title: Mieda Natural Eco Cafe at Nada Natural Farming: Dine at Penang’s magical rustic farmhouse
content:
Written by Nadia Crowe

Penang is known for its delicious street food, incredible artwork, and sandy beaches, but there’s more! Tucked away on the far side of the island is a slower way of life (can you believe it?). Nada Natural Farming plays host to one of the most magical cafes on the island, a place where the landscape is akin to a fairytale movie!

Covered in greenery, and surrounded by towering trees, a post-meal walk is exactly what the doctor ordered for relieving the stress induced by the latest string of events! The kiddos will have a blast playing in the mud, and you’ll be able to enjoy the refreshing smell of nature, and the soothing sounds of the leaves rustling in the wind!

All of the ingredients that go into their yummy western food is grown locally, and though there aren’t many choices on the menu, what they do have is enough for the perfect romantic date!

title: Cliffside resort in the water paradise of Pulau Perhentian: Alunan Resort
content:
The small Perhentian Island is known for its party vibes and we like it that way but for years it’s been hard to find one comfortable and affordable luxury stay that would make you want to stay longer.

Now, years have passed and one, if not THE most spectacular accommodation option you can find on Perhentian Kecil has got to be Alunan Resort – a place where everything is designed to put those all-blue ocean views on full display!

The resort is literally attached to the rocky shores of the island (in fact you’ll need to hike a little to reach your suite), sweeping down to merge with the transparent waters all the while protected by lush surroundings!

Alunan is also the romance spot of choice on the island, owing no doubt to the privacy of its 18 intimate private suites which feature a private rooftop sun deck on the top floor where a hammock, sun loungers and a shaded patio awaits. There are also 2-bedroom options for families!

When not taking a dip in the sea or chowing down on Malaysian and Western cuisine at their onsite Bayu Restaurant, head to Jemari Ayu Spa for pampering treatments or drop by their excursion center and let them take care of your day planning!

TripCanvas’ Tip: If you’re looking for value-for-money dining options, past guests have recommended checking for eateries located along the beach too!

Alunan Resort

Price: 4D3N Snorkelling package from RM 295 per person per night (inclusive of meals, snorkelling and return boat transfers). Special TripCanvas rate: From RM 239.76 per person per night Address: Petani Beach Perhentian Island Kecil, Terengganu Contact: +6016-448 8297 Website

title: Our review of a MUJI-style boutique hotel in Melaka: Muk by Victoria Home
content:
Located 15 minutes’ walk to Jonker Street and 20 minutes to the hipster Kee Ann Food Street, this boutique hotel set in what looks like a historical building is a real showstopper. In fact, passersby often stop over for a few Instagrammable shots by the building. It’s a quirky, creative stay, rather new on the scene too (as of 2019) and it comes at an incredibly affordable price!

When we stepped into the room, we were greeted by contemporary minimalist decor that almost feels like a spacious Muji shop, with extremely calming shades that you’ll be glad to come back to after a day of exploring and eating around Melaka!

We stayed in their Superior Room with two queen beds that can accommodate four adults! It’s super clean and comfy, with all your necessary toiletries provided. What we loved best is that they even have water dispensers in the reading and cafe areas so you don’t need to head out to buy your own bottled water (yes, it’s a pain to always have to remember to do so when travelling, right?).

And you read us right – there’s a lovely reading area where you can just plonk down on the beanbags and pick up a good book from their shelves. Or if like us you are a digital nomad, you can use your laptop in the cafe area which is nice and quiet with good wifi and charging ports – almost like a library (but bring a sweater as it can get pretty cold!).

So if you’re there for more than the weekend (Jonker Street and Kee Ann Food Street are only open on Friday to Sunday nights, so that’s when you can enjoy Melaka in its full vibrance), you can chill or use your devices on weekdays and head out at night to enjoy Melaka’s cafe and local food scene. And on weekends, of course you need to spend the whole day out – that’s when the streets of Melaka truly come to life!

TripCanvas’ Tip: If you’re driving, you could also contact them prior to your arrival to reserve a parking spot, as there are limited parking spaces. And if possible, try to drive a smaller car; we drove an SUV and it was a little bit of a squeeze when parking!

Muk by Victoria Home

title: What to eat to avoid falling sick: 23 foods that boost your immunity
content:
Written by Candice Neo

With the current pandemic situation, besides staying home, I’m sure all of us are doing our best to stay healthy by eating right and staying active.

I’ve been a health nut recently too by feeding everyone with vitamin C with zinc (yes, you cannot leave zinc out because it’s absolutely necessary for a good immune system, and a lack of zinc makes one more susceptible to falling ill).

Of course, we should do our best to find these vitamins and minerals from the natural food that we eat, and consume supplements only if you feel that there’s a lack. But I personally try not to have a strict meal plan to ensure I get all my necessary nutrients for the day (it can be quite tiring!), hence I do take minimal supplements to ensure that at least for the most important ones, I’m not missing them out.

Even so, it’s important to know what we should at least be eating, so without further ado, here’s some food you must include in your daily diet to boost your immunity!

TripCanvas’ Tip: If you are currently on medication, do check with your doctor if any of the food items below clashes with your medicine before you consume them! Do also note that we should only consume what we need. Excessive intake of anything good can lead to health problems too!

1. Citrus fruits rich in vitamin C

Now I’m sure you guys know this! Vitamin C is said to increase the production of our white blood cells which play an important role in protecting our body against infectious diseases.

As someone who falls sick super easily (like 10 times in a year, I’m not kidding), I’ve found that after eating vitamin C supplements regularly, I generally recover from common colds more quickly!

You can find vitamin C in oranges, lemons, grapefruit and limes.

2. Elderberries

Used in medicine for centuries, elderberries and its flowers are packed with antioxidants and have antiviral, anticancer, and anti-inflammatory properties. It is commonly taken as a remedy for colds, flu, and bacterial sinus infections by reducing swelling in mucus membranes.

3. Acai berry

Rich in antioxidants, these berries neutralise free radicals in your body, reduce inflammation and boost your immune system. In fact, it’s so potent an antioxidant that medical researchers are studying it as a potential treatment for cardiovascular diseases, lower rectum cancer, constipation and more!

4. Ginger

Rich in antioxidants, ginger reduces inflammation and boosts your immunity. I strongly recommend making ginger tea (you just need to slice up fresh ginger and boil it in water) at home – it has antibacterial and antiviral properties. One of my aunts makes this at home regularly and her family hardly ever catches the common cold!

5. Garlic

I must admit I’m not a huge fan of garlic, but it is well-known for being rich in antioxidants too and has antibacterial and antiviral properties as well. It helps to protect you against Alzheimer’s disease, heart disease and cancers. It can even shorten the duration of the flu!

6. Red bell peppers (capsicums)

Guess what, red bell peppers actually contain twice as much vitamin C as citrus fruit! It’s also rich in beta-carotene and vitamin A, which are great for the eyes. Vitamin A supports healthy night vision too. It’s also high in antioxidants!

Best to eat it raw though – heat reduces its vitamin C content. Red peppers also increase our metabolism, so yes you can even burn more calories!

Fun fact: Did you know that green peppers are just unripe red peppers? And because they are not fully mature, they contain half as much vitamin C and much less vitamin A than the red version!

7. Mushrooms

High in selenium and B vitamin (such as riboflavin and niacin) content, mushrooms are important for your immune system. They are versatile too – they can be eaten raw mixed in salad, grilled, roasted or sautéed to bring out their flavour.

8. Broccoli

If you must eat only one type of vegetable, it must be broccoli, hands down. There’s a reason why Moms always make us eat our broccoli – it’s packed with so many nutrients that fight cancer! A cup of broccoli gives you as much vitamin C as an orange does. It’s also rich in beta-carotene, potassium, magnesium, zinc, iron B vitamins (B1, B2, B3, and B6) and fibre.

It also contains glutathione, which is an antioxidant.

9. Spinach

Spinach is rich in antioxidants, zeaxanthin and lutein (the latter two are important in protecting our eyes from the damage caused by sunlight. Studies have shown that it also has properties that slow down cancer growth in our bodies.

So eat your spinach like Popeye does! ;)

10. Almond

Did you know that the high antioxidant content in almonds are concentrated in the brown layer of the skin? (Not the shell, which is usually removed from the packets sold, but the skin).

So snacking on these nuts can reduce ageing, inflammation and risks of cancer. It also is rich in vitamin E, which lowers the risk of heart disease and Alzheimer’s disease too.

11. Wheat germ

Wheat germ comes from the innermost part of the wheat kernel, and is actually the most nutritious part of the grain and you can get your daily dose of B vitamins, zinc and VItamin E from here. Sprinkle it onto your yoghurt, cereal or baked desserts as a topping!

12. Green tea

Did you know that drinking certain kinds of Japanese green tea can enable you to lose weight by curbing your desire to snack in between meals? Not to mention it’s full of antioxidants that can boost your immunity and reduce the risk of heart disease. It increases the good cholesterol and decreases bad cholesterol in your body.

Definitely what we need now during this stressful time!

13. Miso soup

Made from fermented soybeans, miso is a salty paste often cooked as soup and is a staple in Japanese meals. And if you, like me, struggle to maintain good gut health, you’ll be glad to know that it’s also rich in probiotics, which will balance the flora and microorganisms in your your gastrointestinal tract.

A healthy gut means a strong immune system too!

14. Pomegranate

Pomegranate has been found to stop the growth of harmful bacteria and have antiviral properties that help prevent flu and other viruses. It promotes the healthy growth of gut flora too which also contributes to a healthy immune system!

I personally enjoy juicing it, or adding it into salad. ;)

15. Low-fat yoghurt

Yoghurt is rich in probiotics, which strengthens gut health, and in term boosts your immunity. It also contains zinc and other minerals that are important for your immune system too!

Other benefits include controlling blood pressure due to its potassium content, helping in workout recovery and weight loss and strengthening your bone density as it contains calcium too.

16. Watermelon

If there’s a fruit juice I would never tire of, it’s watermelon juice. It’s super hydrating and refreshing, not to mention it contains vitamins C, B1, B5, B6 and A, potassium, magnesium and glutathione, which is an antioxidant important for your immune system.

Watermelon also helps to lower your cholesterol level and blood pressure too.

17. Oysters

Oysters are considered a superfood – they contain so much nutrients like selenium, iron, vitamin C, vitamin A and zinc. Some of these are important in building up your immune system.

I love eating them raw but you can grill or stew them too!

18. Papaya

I must admit, I’m not a huge fan of papaya but it does have a host of health benefits, like did you know that one papaya contains more than twice the amount of your daily recommended dose of vitamin C?

You can also get potassium, B vitamins, folate and a digestive enzyme papain which has anti-inflammatory properties from this fruit!

19. Kiwi

Kiwis are full of folate, potassium, vitamin K, and vitamin C, which is essential for your immune system. One cup of kiwi provides around 273 percent of your daily recommended intake of vitamin C! They contain antioxidants too and are high in zeaxanthin and lutein, which help protect your eyes against vision loss.

They help to lower blood pressure too.

20. Sunflower seeds

If you’re a 90’s kid like me, do you recall snacking on chocolate-coated sunflower seeds back in high school? I certainly do and still love them!

Containing vitamin E, magnesium, protein and linoleic fatty acids, sunflower seeds helps to lower blood pressure, cholesterol and blood sugar levels. It also reduces inflammation and reduces risk of heart diseases by lowering blood pressure.

Sprinkle some onto your cereals or yoghurt for that extra crunch! ;)

21. Chicken/Turkey

Have you always heard about how chicken soup is comforting when you are down with the flu? Here’s the facts: Chicken helps to boost the immune cells in your body, while the steam from the soup clears your nasal passages.

And besides being a good source of protein, did you know that eating poultry helps you de-stress? Yes, that’s due to its tryptophan and vitamin B5 content. It also contains magnesium which helps reduce PMS, so ladies, now you know what to look out for when the time of the month comes!

22. Walnuts

Walnuts have antioxidant properties due to its content of vitamin E, melatonin and plant compounds called polyphenols, which can be found in the skin of walnuts. It’s also richer in Omega-3 fat than other nuts, which lowers your risk of heart disease.

Eating walnuts also helps fight inflammation, improves the composition of gut bacteria, lowers blood pressure and may also lower the risk of certain cancers.

23. Carrots

Carrots are a great source of beta-carotene (antioxidants that your body converts into vitamin A, which in turn is important for good vision), antioxidants which reduce the risk of certain cancers, vitamin K1, potassium and fibre.

This root vegetable is known to reduce blood cholesterol levels too!

Are there any other immunity-boosting foods we’ve missed? Share with us! Share these with your loved ones so you know what to look out for when grocery shopping!

Sources: Healthline and related health magazines

title: Visit Tagal Tinopikon Park, an authentic eco-village near Kota Kinabalu (Sabah) where you can feed toothless fish in the river and trek to a waterfall!
content:
Overfishing in Malaysia has resulted in a lack of fish in local rivers, but with the village community effort in Tagal Tinopikon Park (in Notoruss village, Penampang, which is 40 minutes’ drive from Kota Kinabalu city), fishing has been made illegal along the Moyog River here; the name “tagal” means “prohibited” in the local Indigenous Kadazandusun language and anyone who fishes in a tagal area will be fined.

Hence, friendly Pelian (toothless) fish are in abundance and you can even feed them!🥰

We spent half a day chilling at this village, trekked into the rainforest to a waterfall (an easy 30-minute trek!), got creative with batik-painting🎨, watched the villagers do rubber tapping to extract latex from the rubber trees, strolled through the village garden with tropical fruit and indulged in a delicious kampung home-cooked meal prepared by the villagers. 🥬🍍

This was one of my favourite experiences in Sabah, seeing how travellers can bring value to the villagers as they learn about the local community (most proceeds earned go to the villagers), while villagers give travellers a peek into their lives. Creating harmonious and mutually beneficial ecosystems like this plays an important role in the future of sustainable tourism.💚

Special thanks to Tourism Malaysia for inviting us to this Mega Fam trip in 2020, where local and international media were invited to experience exotic Sabah and take part in the PATA Adventure Conference & Mart 2020 that brought together international experts in the adventure travel industry, tourism agencies, adventure product buyers and sellers from across the world.

Tagal Tinopikon Park

Price: RM 5/pax (entrance fee). Tour 💸for 1-4 pax (Totally Borneo Holidays): RM 100/pax (no transport), RM 150/pax (with transport). If you are coming in a bigger group, price would be cheaper per pax. Opening hours: Daily, 10AM - 4PM Location: Penampang, Sabah (Coordinates) Contact: Tour 📞+6016-846 9892 (Alvin, Totally Borneo Holidays) Village 📞+60138646269 / +60165246269 (Ms Regina), for direct booking with the village.

title: Mari-Mari Cultural Village: Step back in time at this indigenous cultural village near Kota Kinabalu (Sabah)
content:
You can’t say you’ve travelled to Sabah without at least getting a glimpse of their local culture! We had a one-stop experience with the major indigenous groups in Sabah at Mari Mari Cultural Village, which is just a 40-minute drive from the city of Kota Kinabalu.🚗

At this interactive village, we met descendents of the indigenous tribes – 🍚 the rice-farmers Kadazandusun, Lundayeh who are hunters and fishermen,🐠 the sea gypsies Bajau 🌊, Rungus people who live in longhouses, and the much-feared headhunter Murut tribe 💀.

We stepped back in time and wandered into their ancient longhouses, took a sip of their local rice wine, indulged in local snacks, saw with our own eyes how they made fire from wood, and were thoroughly entertained by their interactive cultural performances and dances. A local afternoon tea concluded this exotic experience.

This educational tour isn’t so much of a real village but a living museum where indigenous artifacts and traditions are lovingly preserved, curated and showcased, so we as travellers can get a better understanding of each major tribe. Definitely worth a visit if it’s your first time in Sabah!

Special thanks to Tourism Malaysia for inviting us to this Mega Fam trip in 2020, where local and international media were invited to experience exotic Sabah and take part in the PATA Adventure Conference & Mart 2020 that brought together international experts in the adventure travel industry, tourism agencies, adventure product buyers and sellers from across the world.

title: Find magic at this sunset dinner cruise in Kota Kinabalu, Sabah
content:
Sunsets in Kota Kinabalu are not to be missed, and even more so when you can experience it onboard a luxury cruise with dinner! 🍸🍽

🍹Start with drinks on the upper deck and head downstairs for a buffet dinner, complete with live music 🎸serenading you as you dig into satay, fresh prawn, squid, chicken, sushi and a variety of cakes and tropical fruit!🍰🍉🤤🦐

Our tip: Pick a window seat and comfortably wait for that golden moment before you head out for what you are here for – epic sunset snaps as your vessel travels to a lookout spot where you can get an unobstructed view. 😍You can even dine in their VIP room which comes complete with a karaoke system too!🎤

End your night with a local Sabahan dance! 💃🏽

North Borneo Cruises 蓝海之星
